description: Figure 3 presents bright-field (BF) transmission electron microscopy (TEM) images comparing the microstructures of as-solution-treated M1-SS (a) and M2-SS (b) in an Fe–Mn–Al–C austenitic low-density steel. Subfigure (a) shows a grain boundary region in the Ti/V-free M1-SS sample, revealing a clean austenite matrix devoid of visible precipitates. Subfigure (b) displays the Ti/V-modified M2-SS sample, where intragranular nanoscale (Ti,V)C carbides are visible and explicitly indicated by blue arrows and labels within the austenite grains. This direct comparison demonstrates that Ti/V microalloying produces stable (Ti,V)C precipitates that remain in the matrix after solution treatment, contrasting sharply with the precipitate-free M1-SS condition and providing the heterogeneous nucleation sites responsible for localized κ-carbide formation during subsequent aging.
